Chilli__Anthracnos: Remove and destroy infected fruits and plant debris to prevent disease spread.
Improve field sanitation and avoid overhead irrigation to reduce moisture on leaves.
Ensure proper plant spacing for airflow and reduced humidity.
Use disease-free, certified seeds and practice regular field monitoring.
Rotate crops and avoid planting chilli repeatedly in the same field.
Chilli__Damping_Off: Use well-drained, sterile nursery soil to prevent early infection.
Avoid excessive watering and maintain proper nursery ventilation.
Reduce overcrowding in seedling trays to minimize humidity buildup.
Solarize nursery soil before sowing to reduce pathogen load.
Remove weak or infected seedlings immediately.
Chilli___healthy: Maintain good field hygiene and regularly inspect plants to keep them disease-free.
Ensure proper spacing, irrigation, and balanced nutrition to support healthy growth.
Use clean tools and disease-free transplanting material.
Monitor environmental conditions and protect plants during high humidity periods.
Follow crop rotation and avoid continuous chilli cultivation in the same plot.
Chilli__Leaf_Curl_Virus: Remove infected plants early to reduce virus spread.
Manage whitefly population through field hygiene and reflective mulches.
Use virus-free seedlings and resistant varieties if available.
Avoid planting near virus-infected fields and maintain good weed control.
Use physical barriers like insect nets in nurseries.
Chilli__Leaf_Spot: Remove infected leaves and maintain sanitation around plants.
Ensure proper spacing for good air circulation.
Avoid waterlogging and overhead irrigation to reduce leaf wetness.
Use disease-free seeds and rotate crops with non-host plants.
Monitor fields regularly and remove debris after harvest.
Chilli__Veinal_Mottle_Virus: Use certified virus-free seeds to prevent early infection.
Remove infected plants promptly to reduce disease spread.
Control insect vectors using safe cultural practices and field hygiene.
Maintain weed-free borders as many weeds act as virus reservoirs.
Avoid using transplants from unknown or infected fields.
Chilli __Whitefly: Use yellow sticky traps to monitor and reduce whitefly populations.
Remove weeds and plant residues that harbor insects.
Promote good airflow by maintaining appropriate plant spacing.
Protect nurseries with insect-proof netting to prevent early infestation.
Rotate crops and avoid continuous chilli cultivation.
Chilli __Yellowish: Check for nutrient deficiencies and maintain balanced soil fertility.
Improve soil drainage and avoid prolonged waterlogging.
Remove damaged leaves and maintain proper field sanitation.
Inspect for pests or stress factors that may cause yellowing.
Ensure plants receive adequate sunlight and avoid overcrowding.
